leads.name: Amplify Publishing Group
leads.title: Vice President, Production
leads.notes: Herndon, VA hybrid publisher (formerly Mascot Books) producing thought-leadership, business, and children's titles under imprints like RealClear Publishing and 100 Coaches Publishing. Hybrids buy printing continuously for client books, and their post-acquisition growth push makes this a good moment to get on their vendor list.
leads.pitch: Acquired by Civica Media in September 2025 alongside Greenleaf Book Group, with plans to scale production across both brands.

leads.name: Islandport Press
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Yarmouth, Maine regional publisher of New England memoir, outdoors, history, and children's titles founded by Dean Lunt. Regional trade nonfiction in 1,500-5,000 copy runs is classic Cushing-Malloy work, and they manage a growing backlist that needs recurring short-run reprints.
leads.pitch: Fall 2025 list includes 'Always Game' by Christi Elliot and the picture book 'Who Loves Blueberries?', with a larger multi-genre 2026 list announced on their site.

leads.name: AdventureKEEN
leads.title: Publisher & COO
leads.notes: Birmingham, AL independent publisher of outdoor recreation and nature titles under Adventure Publications, Menasha Ridge Press, Wilderness Press, and Nature Study Guides. Field guides and regional hiking guides are text-plus-color paperback work with frequent regional reprints, a strong match for a Midwest offset/digital book manufacturer.
leads.pitch: Expanding Stan Tekiela's Kids' Guide to Birds series (Virginia, October 2025) and relaunching its Nature Study Guides Finder series with new titles like 'Mushroom Finder'; profiled by the Children's Book Council in March

leads.name: Reedy Press
leads.title: Founder / Publisher
leads.notes: St. Louis publisher of city-and-state guidebooks ('100 Things to Do,' 'Secret,' 'Unique Eats') plus commemorative books for organizations. Their high-frequency paperback series model means constant print orders in exactly Cushing-Malloy's run range, and the founder's direct email is published on their contact page.
leads.pitch: Riding the Route 66 centennial with 'Route 66: The First 100 Years' and a steady stream of new 2025-2026 city guides (Jupiter FL, Plano TX, South Carolina).

leads.name: Bushel & Peck Books
leads.title: Founder
leads.notes: Mission-driven children's publisher founded by former Familius exec David Miles, with a buy-one-give-one 'Book-for-Book Promise' that requires steady, cost-efficient reprints. Their board books, picture books, and illustrated nonfiction lines mean frequent short-to-mid runs, and the donation model makes per-unit printing cost a core business lever Cushing-Malloy can speak to directly.
leads.pitch: September 2025 releases include 'Sweet Like Milton Hershey' by Nick Esposito alongside 'I Am the Sun' by Janessa Parker.

leads.name: Brown Books Publishing Group
leads.title: Founder & CEO
leads.notes: Dallas-based relationship-publishing house founded in 1994 by Milli Brown, producing fiction, nonfiction, and children's titles across multiple imprints with in-house distribution. As a 30+ year hybrid that controls its own production pipeline, they are a constant buyer of book manufacturing and a good fit for a reliability-and-quality printer pitch. leads.pitch: Actively publishing into 2026 with a news archive updated March 2026 and a 32-year legacy across its BBPG flagship imprint.

leads.name: TCU Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: Fort Worth press focused on Texas regional history, biography, and literature of the American West -- classic short-run regional nonfiction with cloth and paper editions. Small in-house staff (director, associate editor, production manager) means the director is reachable and manufacturing decisions are centralized.
leads.pitch: One of the oldest Texas university presses (founded 1947), still actively publishing its Texas Biography and Chisholm Trail regional series with a named production manager on staff.

leads.name: Paraclete Press
leads.title: Marketing Director
leads.notes: Brewster, Massachusetts publisher of Christian spirituality, devotionals, poetry-free nonfiction, gift books, and sacred music, distributed by IPG. Cushing-Malloy already prints for numerous faith publishers (a proven vertical), and Paraclete's seasonal Lent/Easter and Advent lines create predictable, recurring print cycles. [Email verification note: receiving server blocks SMTP probes (policy); address sourced from https://paracletepress.com/pages/contact-us.]

leads.name: Matthew Taranto (BitFinity / Brawl in the Family)
leads.title: Creator / Author
leads.notes: Louisiana-based webcomic creator turned book publisher who funded the 'Paper Plates' Paper Mario cookbook on Kickstarter (Oct-Nov 2025, $35K goal) and released the 780-page 'Brawl in the Family: Ultimate Edition' hardcover in Jan 2026. In June 2026 he announced 'Mega Man: Bot in the Family,' an officially licensed comic book with Capcom and NF Publishing headed to Kickstarter. He ships premium hardcovers to thousands of backers per campaign, exactly the recurring short-run offset work Cushing-Malloy handles.
leads.pitch: Announced 'Mega Man: Bot in the Family' (official Capcom collaboration) coming soon to Kickstarter, on the heels of his funded 'Paper Plates' cookbook campaign.

leads.name: M.L. Wang
leads.title: Author
leads.notes: Wisconsin-based indie fantasy author of 'The Sword of Kaigen' and the breakout hit 'Blood Over Bright Haven.' She currently has a Deluxe Illustrated Edition of Blood Over Bright Haven in hosted pre-orders on BackerKit and released a brand-new book, 'Destiny's Edge,' in June 2026. Deluxe illustrated editions require exactly the kind of quality short-run offset printing Cushing-Malloy specializes in; her email is published on her contact page (she notes slow replies, so patience needed).
leads.pitch: Deluxe Illustrated Edition of 'Blood Over Bright Haven' is in pre-order on BackerKit now, and her new book 'Destiny's Edge' launched in June 2026.

leads.name: University of North Georgia Press
leads.title: Managing Editor
leads.notes: Small AUPresses-affiliate press in Dahlonega publishing monographs, nonfiction, regional titles, and print textbooks tied to Georgia's Affordable Learning initiative. Low-volume textbook and monograph printing with reorder cycles is a strong fit for a short-run offset/digital manufacturer.
leads.pitch: Active Affordable Learning Georgia partnership producing print textbooks and OER partner texts, plus a posted 2025-2026 events and internship program.

leads.name: Josh Bernoff (WOBS LLC)
leads.title: Author / Book Coach
leads.notes: Portland, Maine business-book authority, author of 'Build a Better Business Book' and 'Writing Without Bullshit,' who blogs daily and runs an author-services practice (ghostwriting, developmental editing, book proposals) for executive and entrepreneur authors. Many of his clients self-publish and need quality short-run hardcovers for launches, speaking gigs, and bulk corporate sales. One relationship here can feed multiple book-printing projects per year; email published plainly on his contact page.
leads.pitch: Runs an active author-services practice for business-book authors and publishes a daily blog on book publishing at bernoff.com.

leads.name: Anne Byrn (Between the Layers)
leads.title: Author / Food Writer
leads.notes: Nashville-based New York Times bestselling cookbook author ('The Cake Mix Doctor,' 'Baking in the American South') who now runs the popular Between the Layers baking Substack with a large paid readership. She is actively touring, doing signings and lectures (contact email published for booking), and her direct newsletter audience is exactly the base authors use to sell self-published or special-edition print projects. Outreach angle: companion print editions, event stock, and future newsletter-to-book projects.
leads.pitch: Runs the top-tier baking Substack 'Between the Layers' and is actively booking 2026 signings and lectures for 'Baking in the American South.'

leads.notes: Growing library-based press publishing scholarly monographs (modernism, Irish studies, musicology) plus a scholarly journals list and OER imprints -- recurring short-run print work across books and journal issues. leads.name: Sarra Cannon (Heart Breathings)
leads.title: Author
leads.notes: Cedar Hill, Texas indie fantasy author with 25+ titles across eight series and a large author-education following via her Heart Breathings YouTube channel. She just launched 'A Mirror of Shadows' (The Shadow Games Book 1), is writing Book 2, and is doing in-person signings, which drive demand for physical stock. Her big backlist and romantasy audience (a market obsessed with special print editions) make her a strong offset-run candidate; email published for her assistant on her contact page.
leads.pitch: Just launched 'A Mirror of Shadows,' Book One of The Shadow Games series, and announced a return to full-time writing with live signing events.

leads.name: Lisa Bass (Farmhouse on Boone)
leads.source: YouTube / Blog Creator with Book Project
leads.title: Creator
leads.notes: Missouri homesteading and sourdough creator with 700,000+ YouTube subscribers, a blog reaching millions of readers monthly, and the Simple Farmhouse Life podcast. Her sourdough cookbook 'Daily Sourdough' is sold in print through Homestead Living, and she sells courses and downloadable recipe books that are prime candidates for physical editions. Massive audience plus proven appetite for physical cookbooks makes her a high-upside print lead; business email published on her YouTube About page.
leads.pitch: Her print cookbook 'Daily Sourdough' is currently on sale via Homestead Living while her YouTube channel has passed 700,000 subscribers.

leads.name: Doodle and Peck Publishing
leads.title: Founder / Publisher
leads.notes: Family-friendly micro publisher in Yukon, Oklahoma, run by founder Marla F. Jones, producing picture books and chapter books in both hardcover and softcover editions. Their run sizes sit squarely in Cushing-Malloy's 250-5,000 sweet spot, and the owner makes print-buying decisions personally, so outreach reaches the decision-maker immediately.
leads.pitch: Currently promoting new release 'The Perils of Pauline: Where Is President Taft's Cow?' plus new hardcover/softcover editions of 'The Plant Platoon' and 'Just Right!'.

leads.name: Van Raalte Press (Van Raalte Institute, Hope College)
leads.notes: Publishing arm of Hope College's Van Raalte Institute in Holland, MI, producing scholarly regional-history books on Dutch-American immigration, West Michigan history, and missionary memoirs -- small runs sold through the college bookstore and Amazon. A Michigan printer pitch on short-run casebound production and in-state freight savings is a natural fit.
leads.pitch: Just published 'A Clear Vision' by Gordon D. Laman (2026) plus three 2025 titles including 'Present, But Not Counted' and 'Vision, Conflict, and Controversy'.

leads.name: Sarah Biglow
leads.title: Author
leads.notes: Massachusetts-based USA Today bestselling urban fantasy and cozy mystery author who runs recurring Kickstarter campaigns, including 'Guardians of Camelot 3' with special-edition hardcovers featuring foiled cases, jackets, and printed edges. She sells signed print editions direct through her own Shopify store and maintains a dedicated Kickstarter page on her site. Recurring special-edition campaigns plus direct sales make her an ideal fit for short-run premium offset printing.
leads.pitch: Ran the 'Guardians of Camelot' special edition Kickstarter offering foil-stamped hardcovers with printed edges, and lists ongoing Kickstarter campaigns on her website.

Publishes religion, archaeology, education, and the perennially reprinting Andrews Bible Commentary and Andrews Study Bible, meaning recurring reprint volume.
leads.pitch: The Andrews Bible Commentary and Study Bible line remains in active distribution with ongoing reprints, and the press maintains a live storefront and digital catalog.

Just released 'Fury' (End Times Alaska finale) with at least four more titles scheduled through 2027. Longtime indie-community leader who understands unit economics, making him a strong candidate for offset paperback/hardcover runs of his bestselling series for direct and event sales. leads.name: Yeehoo Press
leads.notes: Independent picture-book publisher based in San Pedro/San Diego, CA, publishing English and Chinese editions distributed through Ingram. Full-color picture books in modest runs are exactly the kind of recurring offset work Cushing-Malloy can quote; a domestic Michigan printer is a strong pitch versus overseas color printing given tariff and freight uncertainty.
